title: T.N. govt. permits increased intake in arts and science colleges
content: However, the institutions were barred from seeking sanction for additional posts in view of the increased intake of students

title: Agencies detect rise in gold smuggling after import duty hike
content: A Customs officer said teams rely heavily on profiling, behavioural analysis and intelligence inputs to identify suspects

title: Identity cards for Class 10, 12 students to be issued in first phase: Sengottaiyan
content: As students were required to produce community certificates every year for various purposes, the government has decided to introduce identity cards, which would integrate all student details, the Minister said.

title: Activists flag safety, accommodation lapses after Tiruvallur seafood unit ammonia leak
content: The workers, many of them young women from Odisha and Assam aged between 19 and 25, were off duty and resting in the dormitory when the leak occurred

title: A convert to Islam cannot claim the status of Backward Class Muslim, rules Madras High Court
content: Categorising certain sects as Backward and the remaining as Forward is antithetical to Quranic injunctions. Islam seeks to establish an egalitarian society, says Madurai Bench

title: Centre asks hospitals to disclose post-organ transplant survival data
content: Registered transplant hospitals across the country were told to display the crucial data on the home page of their respective websites

title: UCC to be implemented in State through proper procedure, says Bengal CM
content: Implementation of UCC was promised by Union Home Minister Amit Shah during the campaign for West Bengal Assembly election.

title: Home Minister Amit Shah says next three years decisive in fight against drugs, releases vision document
content: The NDPS Act and Rules will be updated to address emerging challenges and address regulatory gaps while promoting a more reformative approach towards persons suffering from addiction, vision document says

title: Opposition to step up protest against ruling BJP at Thiruavananthapuram Corporation
content: LDF files a complaint to Principal Secretary, alleging swearing-in on Wednesday was not conducted as per norms; UDF's parliamentary party meeting decides to move no-confidence motion against BJP-led administration

title: MDMK’s general body to take a decision on alliance  on Saturday
content: There are expectations that the MDMK may ask its MLAs — T.M. Rajendran and  Senthil Selvan to resign from the membership of the Assembly as they contested on the DMK’s ‘Rising Sun’ symbol in the 2026 election

title: Extension of Kochi Water Metro ferries to Kadamakudy by Onam
content: A floating pontoon for berthing ferries is ready at the Kadamakudy terminal, while the final works on the much-awaited terminal are nearing completion

title: Students in Classes 7, 8, & 9 can continue learning foreign languages this year: Education Ministry sources
content: These students do not have switch to an extra native language till they complete Class 10, the sources added; the clarification comes over a month after CBSE stated that students in Class 9 must study three languages, of which two should be native to India, in the 2026-27 academic year

title: Rahul marks two years as Leader of the Opposition; vows to continue fighting for the people
content: The Opposition leader targets the Prime Minister over his praise of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, saying the students who died due to NEET paper leak are victims of a broken system

title: Drug menace threatens society, future generations must be protected: Kerala CM
content: Kerala government is adopting a systematic approach to curb drug trafficking and dismantle the networks behind it, says Satheesan

title: Ernakulam crosses 1-lakh mark in Census self-enumeration
content: ‘Despite crossing the one-lakh mark, the self-enumeration response in Ernakulam remains well below the district administration’s expectations with just four days left before the June 30 deadline’

title: Permanent TET exemption sought for in-service teachers in A.P.
content: Forum of Registered Teacher Organisations leaders submit representations to MPs, urging them to amend the RTE Act, 2009 to provide the permanent exemption

title: BJP accuses Congress-led govt. in Kerala of promoting alcohol consumption
content: BJP MP and spokesperson Sudhanshu Trivedi described the decision to reduce excise tax on low-alcohol liquor by nearly half as “disastrous”

title: Muharram processions held across Kashmir; CM Omar, L-G Sinha join mourners
content: Shia Muslims hold processions to mark Ashura in Srinagar and other parts of J&K; marches carried out under tight security

title: All eight accused in Ram Temple donation embezzlement case sent to judicial custody
content: Despite rumours that Champat Rai has resigned as general secretary of the Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, no official confirmation yet; arrested accused include close aides of Mr. Champat Rai

title: Death toll in Kolkata warehouse collapse rises to 16
content: Rescue operations are being carried out for the third consecutive day at the site of the accident, by NDRF, Indian Army, Kolkata Police and other agencies

title: Civil society group proposes water security commission amid concerns over shrinking waterbodies in Kashmir
content: Centre’s clearances for felling nearly 2.8 million trees, coupled with large-scale infrastructure in hill regions, could destabilise fragile mountain ecosystems, says the chairman of the civil society group

title: U.P. Rajeev appointed Director of Vikram Sarabhai Space Centre
content: Dr. Rajeev has extensive experience in the area of rocket control and guidance, and has led the mission designs and mission simulations of major launch vehicles of the ISRO

title: MOOCs workshop to strengthen digital higher education in A.P.
content: Five-day workshop on design, development and delivery of Massive Open Online Courses to begin on July 29

title: PM Museum and Library to mark 105th birth anniversary of late former PM Narasimha Rao
content: The event on June 27 which will include an exhibition showcasing a collection of rare photographs, official documents, speeches, correspondence and archival material tracing the journey of the late former PM.

title: Huge crowds throng Bara Shaheed Dargah to celebrate ‘Rottela Panduga’ in Nellore
content: MA&UD Minister says that around 15 lakh devotees are expected to visit this year; as part of the special arrangements, 6,315 sanitation staff, 1,870 police personnel, 45 expert swimmers, and eight boats have been deployed

title: Watch: Eight arrested over Ram temple donations scandal | Above the Fold | 26.06.2026
content: From arrests over the alleged embezzlement of Ram temple donations and a standoff between Nihang Sikhs and the police in Uttarakhand, to the release of the names of servicemen who died during Operation Sindoor, the rising death toll from Venezuela’s earthquakes, revised fuel prices, higher Apple product prices, FIFA highlights, and more.

title: Myanmar replaces Afghanistan as key opium source, impact seen on India’s eastern border: NCB
content: Myanmar’s Golden Triangle has expanded as an opiate supplier and meth hub, with the drugs then flowing across a porous border into Manipur, Mizoram, and Nagaland; drone-based drug trafficking from Pakistan is also a rising concern

title: Transfer guidelines sought for government degree college lecturers in A.P.
content: Akhil Bharatiya Rashtriya Shaikshik Mahasangh submits representations to HRD Minister Nara Lokesh and senior officials of the Higher Education Department

title: Tourists continue dangerous acts on road in Munnar
content: Despite repeated warnings and police cases, they continue riding atop vehicles and with doors open on the winding roads of the hill town; viral video shows a group of youths riding their SUV dangerously near Chithirapuram

title: Archaeological Survey discovers 25 rare inscriptions in Markapuram district of Andhra Pradesh
content: The ASI team has taken estampages of 25 inscriptions dating from the 2nd century to the 15th century and is now in the process of digitising those inscriptions in several languages

title: Congress ‘surrendered’ Katchatheevu to Sri Lanka without consulting stakeholders: BJP
content: BJP spokesperson Sudhanshu Trivedi describes it a ‘black spot’ in the ‘Congress’s history of surrenders’

title: Married to an Indian, JD Vance tries to make India-hater Nixon great again
content: US Vice President JD Vance's recent praise for Richard Nixon, a president known for his animosity towards India and denigration of Indian women, has sparked controversy. Vance lauded Nixon as a "political genius" whose reputation deserves a "renaissance," downplaying the Watergate scandal. This comes at a time when US-India relations are already strained, highlighting an awkward historical disconnect for Indian readers.

title: 'Killing Ketan was easier than confronting my family': Siya Goyal's chilling revelation to Pune cops
content: A shocking revelation emerged in the Lohagad Fort murder case as accused Siya Goyal reportedly told police she disliked her fiancé, Ketan Agarwal, due to his hair patch. Investigators are probing her brother's knowledge of her affair with co-accused Chetan Chaudhary. Financial disparities are believed to have influenced the engagement, with Siya allegedly finding murder easier than disappointing her family.

title: 920 dead, over 3,000 injured: Venezuela quake toll rises as rescuers race to find survivors
content: Venezuela grapples with a devastating toll of 920 dead and nearly 3,360 injured following twin earthquakes. La Guaira state is severely impacted, with thousands missing and millions potentially affected. International rescue teams and aid, including India's 'Operation Amistad' and US assistance, are now arriving to support desperate search-and-rescue efforts amidst widespread destruction and fear of aftershocks.

title: World champions' curse strikes again as India suffer historic Ireland shock
content: India's T20 World Cup reign began with a shock 34-run defeat to Ireland in Belfast, continuing a bizarre trend of recent champions losing their first T20I post-victory. Ireland's bowlers, including debutants, stifled India's chase after a competitive 182. This historic win for Ireland marks a disappointing start for new Indian captain Shreyas Iyer and extends an unusual statistic for T20 World Cup holders.

title: NY Mayor Zohran Mamdani files motion to ban delivery app Motoclick: What lawsuit says
content: New York City is seeking to ban food delivery platform Motoclick for allegedly paying workers as little as $3.67 per hour, a fraction of the mandated $22.13 minimum wage. City officials filed a motion for a preliminary injunction, aiming to halt the company's operations unless it complies with labor laws.

title: Bill Gates admits to the House panel that he had at least three extramarital affairs
content: Bill Gates revealed to Congress that Jeffrey Epstein attempted to leverage knowledge of Gates' extramarital affairs to re-establish contact after Gates sought to end their association. While no direct threats were made, Epstein allegedly used intermediaries for veiled warnings and explored blackmail tactics. Gates acknowledged past affairs, emphasizing Epstein's lack of involvement and that he only learned of them post-professional ties.

title: 'You can't just turn up and win': Iyer's blunt message after Ireland shocker
content: Ireland secured a stunning 34-run victory over India in the opening T20I, taking a 1-0 series lead. Despite a strong start with the ball, India's bowlers faltered in the middle and death overs, allowing Ireland to post 182. Captain Shreyas Iyer acknowledged the team's lapse in execution and vowed a strong comeback in the next match.

title: Investor Michael Burry repeats warning on Palantir after calling stock ‘a sand castle’
content: Renowned investor Michael Burry has expressed serious concerns about Palantir Technologies, likening its stock to a "sand castle" amid a significant downturn.  He noted a "new lower low" with declining trading volume, suggesting a prolonged downtrend.  This comes as Palantir, once an AI darling, faces investor skepticism over its valuation.  Meanwhile, Burry has increased his stake in China's Alibaba, seeing it as an undervalued AI leader.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for June 27
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for June 27, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: 'Foolish violation of ceasefire deal': Trump says Iran shot 4 drones at ships crossing Hormuz
content: US President Donald Trump condemned alleged Iranian drone attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz, calling them a "foolish violation of our Ceasefire Agreement." Iran's IRGC Navy asserted control over transit routes, warning vessels to follow Tehran's directives. This follows technical talks between the US and Iran aimed at de-escalating tensions and ensuring safe passage for commercial ships.

title: Iyer joins Kohli, Pant & Gill on unwanted list after Ireland script historic win
content: Shreyas Iyer's captaincy debut for India ended in a disappointing 34-run loss to Ireland in Belfast, marking his entry into an unwanted record book. Ireland secured their historic first-ever win over India in men's international cricket, thanks to strong performances from their debutants. Despite Iyer's own impressive pre-match statistics, the team's chase faltered, falling short of Ireland's 182.

title: Travis Kelce lookalike wanted in purse theft case sparks viral frenzy
content: The Salem Police Department is searching for a man accused of stealing a purse and using the victim's credit cards after surveillance photos showed a suspect who closely resembles Chiefs star Travis Kelce. In a viral Facebook post, police joked about the resemblance and referenced Taylor Swift, writing, "Na, it can’t be. He’s a Swifty. He wouldn’t do this." Meanwhile, the real Kelce was in Nashville hosting Tight End University alongside a surprise appearance from his fiancée, Taylor Swift.

title: Brittany Mahomes gets emotional over motherhood's life-changing impact
content: Brittany Mahomes shared that becoming a mother to Sterling, Bronze, and Golden has completely changed her outlook on life. She said motherhood made her more intentional, purposeful, and motivated to inspire her children to dream big and work hard. Brittany now prioritizes opportunities that align with her family values. Patrick Mahomes has also spoken about balancing his NFL career with being a devoted husband and father, emphasizing that family always comes first.

title: Rangers trade Brett Berard to the Montreal Canadiens for William Trudeau
content: The New York Rangers traded Brett Berard to the Montreal Canadiens for William Trudeau on June 26, ending a stalled fit for the 23-year-old forward. The deal gives Berard a cleaner path in Montreal and gives New York a left-shot defenseman after moving a player who faced waiver pressure and limited NHL runway.

title: TN minister's old video sparks drug use claims; says he was crushing tablet for daughter
content: Tamil Nadu Minister D. Sarathkumar has vehemently denied drug use allegations following the resurfacing of a two-year-old video. He clarified that the footage, showing him arranging a powder, depicted him crushing a tablet for his daughter. Sarathkumar dismissed the claims as politically motivated and baseless, asserting his readiness to cooperate with any investigation into the matter.

title: 'Political rivals, not enemies': BJP downplays Fadnavis, Uddhav travelling on same flight
content: Speculation arose after Maharashtra chief minister Devendra Fadnavis and Shiv Sena (UBT) supremo Uddhav Thackeray were seen on the same flight.  Despite political differences, BJP MLC Pratap Lad downplayed the encounter, emphasizing cordial relations and noting Thackeray's attendance at his daughter's wedding. Lad also expressed surprise at Thackeray using a commercial flight, while denying BJP involvement in recent Shiv Sena (UBT) Lok Sabha MP defections.

title: Khushbu Sundar shares first wedding pictures of daughter Avantika
content: Actor-politician Khushbu Sundar's family has a new reason to celebrate. Her elder daughter, Avantika Sundar, married Shravan Sreenivasan in an intimate wedding ceremony held in Goa on Thursday evening. The private affair was attended by family members and a close-knit circle of friends from the Indian film industry.

title: Ireland stun T20 world champions India to script historic first-ever victory
content: Ireland secured a historic 34-run victory over reigning T20 World Champions India in the series opener. Despite India's strong start, Ireland's disciplined pace attack and a crucial counter-attacking partnership in the death overs proved decisive. Lorcan Tucker's fifty and a late surge from Gareth Delany and George Dockrell propelled Ireland to 182, a total India couldn't chase, falling for 148.

title: Why India making its own bullet trains is a big deal - explained
content: The trains that will run on India’s first bullet train corridor will be manufactured indigenously. And that will be a feat for India’s engineering capabilities. A Rs 866.87 crore contract has been awarded to BEML Limited by Integral Coach Factory (ICF) Chennai for manufacturing two prototype bullet train or high-speed train sets.

title: Volkswagen may cut 1 lakh jobs amid China competition, tariff pressure
content: Volkswagen is reportedly considering a massive overhaul, including closing four German factories and significantly increasing job cuts, potentially impacting up to 100,000 roles. This drastic move comes as the automaker faces intense competition from Chinese rivals, US tariffs, and declining European demand. The proposals, set for discussion soon, are expected to face strong opposition from unions and a key state shareholder, highlighting the immense pressure on the German giant.

title: Abhishek Sharma scripts T20I history, sets unmatched record with blazing fifty
content: Abhishek Sharma etched his name in T20I history, smashing a 19-ball fifty against Ireland, his fifth half-century off 20 or fewer deliveries. His explosive 50, featuring seven fours and two sixes, propelled India's chase after pacers restricted Ireland to 182. Though dismissed shortly after his milestone, Sharma's fearless innings cemented his status as a destructive opener.

title: Prasidh's nightmare return! India pacer registers unwanted T20I record
content: Prasidh Krishna's return to India's T20I squad was marred by a costly spell against Ireland, where he conceded 27 runs in a single over, the most by an Indian against the hosts. Despite a strong start by India's bowlers, Ireland's middle order, led by Lorcan Tucker and Gareth Delany, fought back impressively. This resilience propelled Ireland to a competitive 182/9, leaving Prasidh with two unwanted records.

title: Watch: Car-sized aircraft crashes into Beijing's tallest skyscraper; police seal off area
content: A car-sized aircraft reportedly struck Beijing's tallest skyscraper, the 108-storey CITIC Tower, on Friday evening, causing damage to two glass panels. Bystanders described a loud crash, and authorities quickly cordoned off the area, preventing filming. Social media posts about the incident were reportedly removed. The official cause and details remain unconfirmed by authorities.

title: ‘No one saw it happen’: Siya's lawyer claims no proof linking her to Ketan's death
content: A lawyer for Siya Goyal, accused in her fiancé's death at Lohagad Fort, asserts police lack direct evidence and eyewitnesses. While the case shifted from accidental death to murder, Goyal's defense claims no corroborating proof of her involvement. Investigators, however, cite extensive call records and CCTV footage suggesting a conspiracy between Goyal and her alleged boyfriend, Chetan Chaudhary, who is also in custody. Both deny the allegations.

title: Former Trump adviser John Bolton pleads guilty to mishandling classified files
content: Prosecutors accused Bolton of sharing sensitive government information with two relatives for possible inclusion in a memoir he was writing. The material allegedly included notes from intelligence briefings as well as meetings involving senior US officials and foreign leaders. Bolton had pleaded not guilty to 18 criminal charges filed against him last year.

title: Rajasthan-born Ireland pacer makes history, becomes only 2nd Irish player to ...
content: Playing his first international match against India in Belfast on Friday, Moondra struck with the first delivery of his spell in the second over of India's innings.

title: 120 against thousands: The incredible story of the Battle of Longewala
content: Amidst the chaos of the 1971 war, 120 valiant Indian soldiers stationed at Longewala faced an overwhelming assault by 4,000 Pakistani troops supported by more than 40 tanks. Their indomitable will through the night not only held the line but allowed the Indian Air Force to inflict devastating losses at dawn, marking a critical moment of exceptional leadership and cooperative warfare.

title: 'What is going on in madrasas?' UP dy CM questions Babri Masjid donations amid Ram Temple row
content: Uttar Pradesh Deputy Chief Minister Brajesh Pathak downplayed the Ram Mandir donation controversy, questioning why funds for the Babri Masjid aren't scrutinized. He accused opposition parties of appeasement politics and attacking Sanatan culture to win Muslim votes. Pathak also alleged illegal activities and terror funding in madrasas. An FIR has been lodged against eight individuals in the Ram Temple donation case, with an SIT investigating the matter.

title: Birthday boy Shivam Dube joins elite club, becomes only the fourth Indian to...
content: Shivam Dube celebrated his 33rd birthday with a remarkable T20I wicket on his special day against Ireland in Belfast. He became the fourth Indian bowler to achieve this rare feat, joining an elite club of Indian stars. Dube's impactful spell contributed to India's performance in the high-scoring match, showcasing his growing value as a dual-role player.

title: Who is Jai Moondra? Ireland's Rajasthan-born pacer makes T20I debut against India
content: Born in Tonk, Rajasthan, Moondra moved to Ireland in 2021 to pursue a Master's degree in Electronics and Communication. After arriving, he joined Leinster Cricket Club in Dublin and worked his way into Ireland's domestic setup.

title: False alarm: Dubai residents receive missile alert, told to 'disregard' it minutes later
content: Dubai residents were briefly alarmed by a missile alert on Friday, only for authorities to quickly retract the warning, urging them to disregard it. This marks the first such alert since the Iran war's ceasefire. The UAE's Foreign Minister later spoke with his Iranian counterpart, emphasizing diplomatic dialogue for regional stability amidst ongoing peace deal negotiations.

title: Pakistan's 2017 Champions Trophy star to play ENG's T20 blast as 'domestic player'
content: Nottinghamshire, currently second in the North Group after six matches, expects Amir to be available for Friday's T20 Blast fixture against Kent as the team looks to strengthen its push for the knockout stages.

title: Repeated thefts, CCTV clips, and family ties: Inside Ram Temple donation 'embezzlement' probe
content: A Special Investigation Team (SIT) probe into alleged theft from Ayodhya's Ram Janmabhoomi temple donation boxes has led to eight arrests and the recovery of over ₹79 lakh. CCTV footage reportedly shows an accused repeatedly stealing cash, with allegations of a cover-up involving a relative of a co-accused. Trust leadership has resigned, and Chief Minister Adityanath vows no one will be spared in the ongoing investigation.

title: Watch: Boeing 777's near-miss — wing skims inches from runway during risky manoeuvre
content: The widely shared footage has fuelled debate among aviation experts and pilots, many of whom questioned the unusually low altitude at which the widebody aircraft was flown. The Boeing 777 is one of the world's largest twin-engine commercial aircraft and is generally operated with generous safety margins during low-level manoeuvres because of its size, weight and performance characteristics.

title: 'Sarpanch' Shreyas breaks Ro-Ko's record in 1st game as India captain, becomes ...
content: When he walked out for the toss against Ireland in Belfast on Friday, Iyer had already captained 114 T20 matches across franchise and domestic cricket — the most by an Indian before taking charge of the national side in the format.

title: Pak dissident Mahrang Baloch shares her prison reading list, says books became her strength
content: Mahrang Baloch, a prominent activist for Baloch rights, has been sentenced to life imprisonment in Pakistan on terrorism charges. Baloch, who joined the movement seeking her allegedly killed father, leads the Baloch Yakjehti Committee, campaigning against enforced disappearances. Despite facing solitary confinement and health issues, she finds strength in books, drawing inspiration from figures like Nelson Mandela and Indira Gandhi.

title: Wig conspiracy behind Ketan's murder? Father says Siya Goyal’s family knew about his hair patch
content: The father of murdered Pune realtor Ketan Agarwal met the Maharashtra CM, demanding the death penalty for the accused. The CM assured a fast-tracked investigation and an SIT formation. Police revealed the victim was pushed from Lohagad Fort after the accused allegedly feared family disrepute if they eloped. Both suspects have confessed to the conspiracy and murder.

title: 'Unfortunately, no': Iyer confirms Sooryavanshi will have to wait for India debut
content: Young batting prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will have to wait for his historic India debut. Captain Shreyas Iyer confirmed the 15-year-old was not in the playing XI for the first T20I against Ireland, citing team balance.  Despite the delay, Sooryavanshi, who had a stellar IPL and India A season, remains on the cusp of becoming India's youngest international cricketer.

title: Telangana 2BHK housing scheme: Who gets a free house and how the selection works
content: Telangana's Double Bedroom Housing Scheme offers fully constructed, free homes to the underprivileged and homeless communities. Each residence spans 560 sq ft, complete with necessary amenities, lifting financial constraints for recipients. Focused on Below Poverty Line families, the initiative ensures ample reservations for Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, and minorities, employing a detailed multi-step verification and selection process for fair distribution.

title: 152 posts sanctioned to strengthen family health centres
content: Posts include Medical Officer and Nursing Officer. Vacancies will be reported to the PSC and appointments made from the existing rank lists

title: Punjab BJP Chief Praises Maharashtra's Move To Consult Sikhs On Takht Law
content: His remarks came as Punjab leaders, including former Union minister Harsimrat Kaur Badal, Congress MP Sukhjinder Singh Randhawa and Akali Dal Waris Punjab De leader Manpreet Singh Ayali, opposed the proposed law.

title: Israel To Remain In South Lebanon Until Hezbollah Disarms: Netanyahu
content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Friday that Israel would remain in south Lebanon until Hezbollah disarms, shortly after the announcement in Washington of a framework agreement with the United States and Lebanon.

title: Netherlands Euthanises Sick Child Under 12 For First Time In History
content: The Netherlands introduced a legal change in 2024 allowing euthanasia for children aged between 1 and 12 who are facing severe and unbearable suffering from a terminal illness.

title: In Breakthrough, Lebanon, Israel And US Announce Trilateral Agreement
content: The agreement "begins to put in place a framework for lasting peace and security," US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said.

title: 42 Ships Cross Strait Of Hormuz Using Oman Passage Despite Iran Threats
content: Traffic levels in the Strait of Hormuz fell back from Wednesday's high, but vessels continued to use a non-Iranian-approved passage despite a ship being struck by a projectile, tracking platforms showed.

title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.6 Series in US-Only Preview At Government's Request
content: By securing early access to frontier models, US officials are aiming to identify threats ranging from cyberattacks to military misuse before the tools are widely deployed.

title: Israel Declassifies Documents On Daring 1976 Entebbe Hostage Rescue
content: The operation saw dozens of Israeli commandos storm the Entebbe Airport, where Palestinian and German militants - backed by Ugandan forces - had taken 106 passengers from a hijacked flight from Tel Aviv to Paris.

title: UAE Discusses Hormuz Security With Iran In Rare Call Amid Ceasefire
content: The exchange, reported by state news agency WAM, marks a rare public contact between Abu Dhabi and Tehran following tensions linked to the US-Israeli war on Iran.

title: Bengal To Implement Uniform Code, Bring Anti-Conversion Law: Suvendu Adhikari
content: Addressing a programme at Rabindra Sadan to mark the 150th anniversary of 'Vande Mataram', Suvendu Adhikari alleged that infiltration had led to demographic changes and posed a threat to the state's social fabric and national security.

title: Celebrations To Mourning: Bihar Groom Dies Just Before Doing Rounds Of Fire
content: Shakti Nath Sharma, 25, was to be married to Raghuni Kumari, daughter of Santosh Sharma, from Chikni village in the East Champaran district.

title: Guilty Should Be Jailed: VHP Chief On Ram Temple Donation Row
content: Speaking to NDTV, Alok Kumar said the most important step now is to ensure that the FIR is investigated quickly and fairly and that all those found guilty are brought to justice.

title: Man Arrested For Sexually Assaulting 8-Year-Old Girl UP's Meerut: Cops
content: Police in Meerut have arrested an accused wanted in connection with the alleged sexual assault of an eight-year-old girl in the Sadar Bazar area of Meerut.

title: Venezuela Earthquakes Live: Deaths From Venezuela Earthquakes Climbs To At Least 920 Dead
content: Venezuela earthquake LIVE Updates: The second earthquake struck just a minute later, with a 7.5-magnitude, and its epicentre was 16 kilometres southwest of Moron.

title: Student Risks Life To Rescue Stranded Dog During Arunachal Flash Flood
content: Amid devastation caused by a flash flood in Arunachal Pradesh's Keyi Panyor district, a Class XI student risked his life to rescue a stranded dog from raging waters.

title: German City Sizzles At 41.3 Degrees, Country's Highest-Ever Temperature
content: The extreme forecast has already prompted the cancellation of numerous events over the weekend, including concerts, festivals and sporting competitions.

title: Devendra Fadnavis Called, Asked Me To Take Up Case: Ujjwal Nikam On Fort Murder
content: Ujjwal Nikam, who was the special public prosecutor in the 26/11 Mumbai terror attacks trial, told NDTV that the Chief Minister had explained the facts of the case to him during the call.

title: Rs 7,744 Crore A Year: Indian-Origin CEO Highest-Paid Executive After Musk
content: Shankh Mitra's rise began in Kolkata, where he studied engineering at Jadavpur University, completing a Bachelor of Engineering degree in Instrumentation and Electronics Engineering.

title: UK Breaks Record Hottest June Day Record For 3rd Time This Year
content: The 36.9 degrees Celsius record temperature was chalked in Wattisham, Suffolk, in southeast England, the meteorologists said, breaking the previous high of 36.7 degrees Celsius set on Thursday.

title: UK farms set to trial fertiliser invented by two PIOs
content: the liquid bio-fertiliser known as “Reclaimer” helps crops withstand extreme conditions such as flooding and drought without the use of harsh chemicals . it was developed by two Indians from Kerala who founded an agri-biotech company in Britain .

title: PoK man caught near LoC
content:
JAMMU: Alert Army troops Friday apprehended a PoK resident near LoC in Poonch district, sources said. “A man was seen moving suspiciously near a forward area along LoC and was promptly apprehended by alert Army troops during routine surveillance,” they said. “The individual has been identified Mohammed Sajad (25), son of Mohammed Rafiq, a resident of Abasspur in PoK,” they added. The man is being questioned to ascertain his motive to cross the LoC.

End of Article
